column9: A Blue Start: A large-scale pairwise and higher-order social network dataset (Controlled Download); Beyond the Hashtags: #Ferguson, #Blacklivesmatter, and the Online Struggle for Offline Justice (Virtual Data Enclave); ChatGPT in education: A discourse analysis of worries and concerns on social media (Virtual Data Enclave); Meta Ad Targeting Dataset; Meta Content Library and Content Library API; Politweets: Tweets of politicians, celebrities, news media, and influencers from India and the United States (Virtual Data Enclave); Replication data for "Emergent structures of attention on social media are driven by amplification and triad transitivity" (Controlled Download)"; State of Social Connections Study (Controlled Download); U.S. 2020 Facebook and Instagram Election Study (Virtual Data Enclave)

column10: The Applicant's email address must show their institutional affiliation. Example: emailaddress@umich.edu, emailaddress2@pewresearch.org
The Lead Researcher's email address must show their institutional affiliation. NOTE: Personal email addresses, such as @gmail.com, are not permitted.

Please name the highest degree earned by the Lead Researcher (e.g., PhD, MD, DrPh, JD, MA, MS, MPH, BA, etc.). NOTE: this information is collected for demographic purposes; no particular degree is required to access the data.

column2: Affiliational profile URL (organization, research, or academia)
column10: The Lead Researcher is required to provide a personal profile showcasing their current affiliation with their organization. Please include a link to any of the following: an active institutional profile or biography, organizational website with directory or staff list, ORCID or ResearchGate profile, or a webpage for their research group, lab, or department. If providing these links is not feasible, other documentation may be submitted at the end of the application to verify the researcher’s affiliation.  [[NOTE: LinkedIn profiles, GitHub Pages, or personal websites are not permitted.]]

Please provide the URL of the Lead Researcher's publicly accessible personal Facebook profile page. If they do not have a Facebook profile, they will need to create one to obtain access to the tools. Organizational profiles or business Pages are not allowed.
The Lead Researcher must be currently affiliated with the institution provided in this field.

column2: Are there any collaborators that need access to the requested data for this research project?
column10: Please select 'Yes' only if your collaborators will perform any type of analysis or data management activities.  Adding Collaborators: Collaborators can be added at any time after the Lead Researcher’s application has been submitted. A separate form must be completed for each collaborator. The following information is required: Lead Researcher’s application ID (SOMARAPPLY-####) Research project title Lead Researcher’s last name and email address To add collaborators, use the Collaborator Addition Request Form and consult the guidance document for detailed instructions.

Provide the email addresses of anyone who should receive updates about this application. These individuals will receive the same notifications as you. Use commas to separate multiple addresses (e.g., john.doe@university.edu, jane.smith@university.edu).

column2: Will your research contribute to the detection, identification, and understanding of systemic risks in the European Union?

column10: In 250 words or less (up to 1,750 characters), summarize your research agenda or plans in a way that a non-specialist can understand. Your response should clearly outline your area of focus, key research questions, and methodologies. If relevant, include any endpoints, data types, or specific data fields of interest. This summary should provide enough detail to demonstrate how your research aligns with the data you are applying for. Before applying, review the publicly available documentation to ensure the data meets your needs.
List the keywords associated with the research agenda. Please separate the keywords with commas.
List the funding sources that will support the Lead Researcher's data analysis. Please separate the funding items with commas. Please enter "N/A" if there are none.

column2: Justification for access to requested data
column10: In 250 words or less (up to 1,750 characters), briefly explain why this dataset is essential for your research. Specify which variables, fields, or aspects of the data are needed to answer your research questions or achieve your objectives.

column2: Evidence of responsible experience or use of sensitive or restricted data
column10: Evidence of responsible experience or use of sensitive or restricted-use datasets is required. Please provide up to 3 citations or examples of your research that demonstrate your experience using sensitive data.  *Character limit: 5000.

column2: Ethical guidelines to be used to guide research project
column10: Please 1) indicate the ethical guidelines you will use to guide your research project, and 2) describe how you plan to apply specific principles in practice. For example, you may refer to the AOIR Ethics, NeurIPS Code of Ethics, or Williams, Burlap, and Sloan (2017), but you must also describe how you adhere to the guidelines you selected.

column2: Upload signed Restricted Data Use Agreement*  For access to the Meta Content Library API in SOMAR's Virtual Data Enclave, the Restricted Data Agreement must be reviewed and then signed and dated by the Lead Researcher and their institution's legal representative or signatory. Please download the agreement document and upload the signed and dated version back on this form (see below).  To expedite the application process for earlier UI access, applications may be submitted without the agreement for initial reviews by SOMAR staff and then updated with signed agreements uploaded to the form, either by the applicants or SOMAR staff.  Only ICPSR’s and Meta’s agreements can be used for access to the data products; Data Use Agreements from researchers’ institutions are not likely to be accepted for this application.
column10: To expedite the application process, applications may be submitted without the Restricted Data Use Agreement. If you do not have the document ready to upload, please upload a Word document with a note to indicate you are working on obtaining a Restricted Data Use Agreement. If approved, applicants will still receive UI access, and once the signed agreement is uploaded and approved by SOMAR staff, they will be granted access to the API.
